main_event: Crew of B-25 on night armed reconnaissance over northwest Korea at 0225Z on 3 April 1953 observed bluish-green object (turning red, then disappearing) approximately 200 feet from aircraft on parallel level flight path; four crew members observed for 3-10 seconds each.
sighted_object: bluish-green turning to red; approximately 200 feet from aircraft; level parallel flight with aircraft; rapid descent; approximately 5 feet wide; resembled a meteor; sparks observed; changed color: bluish-green to red (Dillon), white (Perry), bluish to green to red (Vermillion), green to red to white (Caruthers)
witness_description: Lt. Colonel John D. Dillon (B-25 pilot, heading 355 degrees, 200 mph, 5,000 ft altitude, on top of solid overcast); 1/Lt Syris D. Perry; 1/Lt Frederick J. Vermillion (navigator); A/2C William H. Caruthers (engineer); all of 3d Bomb Group Mission #3-26909
interesting_points: Four crew members observed object with different durations: Dillon ~10 sec, Perry ~5 sec, Vermillion ~10 sec, Caruthers ~3 sec; record card records '5 Seconds'
Object appeared approximately 200 feet from aircraft on level, parallel flight with same heading; may have been much farther given difficulty of night distance estimation

Color change sequence differed between observers (bluish-green to red; white; bluish-green-red; green-red-white); attributed to composition, atmospheric refraction, and cockpit/navigation light effects
Moon entering third quarter at time of sighting — may have enhanced visibility
Report evaluation: B-1 (possibly more reliable than most); approving officer doubted object was projectile from aircraft or ground weapon
CSV flag C:dur_meta=15.0s_vs_form=300.0s was haiku error; form clearly states '5 Seconds'
